name: Paleo Morning Glory Bread

ingredients.item: • 65 grams of heated coconut oil (a third of a cup)
• a quarter teaspoon of Celtic sea salt
• 60 grams dried cranberries, raisins, or chopped dates
• 46 grams, 1/2 cup shredded or grated carrots
grams 1/2 cup finely chopped apples or pears
• 1/2 cup walnuts, chopped (46 grammes + extra for

method: Preheat the oven to 350 degrees Fahrenheit. Set aside a bread
pan greased with coconut oil or lined with parchment paper.
Melt the coconut oil and set it aside while you finish the other
ingredients.
Sift together almond flour, tapioca flour, baking powder, baking
soda, cinnamon, and salt in a medium mixing basin.
Toss the flour mixture with cranberries, raisins, or dates. To
keep the dry fruit from sticking together, mix it. Remove from
the equation.
Peel and core the carrots and apples.
Whisk eggs in a mixing basin with a hand whisker. In a mixing
dish, combine the melted coconut oil and coconut sugar.
Combine until all of the components are well combined.
Using a rubber spatula, fold in the carrots and apples.
Combine the wet and dry ingredients. With a rubber spatula,
gradually mix in the dry ingredients until they are well
integrated. Don't overmix the ingredients.
Mix in the walnuts. Inside a large bowl, mix all of the
ingredients until they are equally spread. Make sure the
elements aren't overmixed.
Fill the loaf pan halfway with the thick batter.
Then, with a spatula, smooth out the top.
If desired, garnish with chopped walnuts.
(optional)
Bake 45-48 minutes, or until a toothpick
inserted in the center comes out clean.
Take the pan out of the oven and set it aside for
at least 15 minutes before removing it.
Set aside for another 30 minutes before cutting.
